## zsh
- Configure your shell
- Fresh install (recommended): cp .zshrc.setup ~/.zshrc
- Existing .zshrc: add the REQUIRED TOOL-SPECIFIC ENTRIES section from .zshrc.setup to your config
- Set zsh as your default shell: chsh -s $(which zsh)
- Log out and back in for the change to take effect

## zellij
- Zellij will autostart on SSH login once .zshrc.setup is sourced
- To enable the web terminal, add to ~/.config/zellij/config.kdl: start_web_server true
- Or enable it from the session manager (Ctrl o + s)
- Create a login token (shown once — save it): zellij web --create-token
- Point your reverse proxy at http://localhost:8082

## openssh-server
- After confirming key-based login works, disable password auth
- Create /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/99-hardening.conf and add: PasswordAuthentication no
- Reload sshd: sudo systemctl reload ssh
